other_observers: Mark Peterson and finder, Marty Wolf; Marty Wolf
specific_location: Marty Wolf's house in NW Colorado Springs, just west of 30th St. in scrub oak habitat in a newish foothill neighborhood.
description: This was a large and chunky Zonotrichia sparrow with a longish tail, partial golend crown (without solid dark on crown, rusty edges to tertials and greater coverts, bi-colored bill with upper "mandible" appearing grayer than "lower mandible" which was lighter gray with pinkish tones. Legs appeared dull pink.Behaviors: Mostly a skulker, coming on to the edge of an open deck for food, the retreating quickly into the dense vegetation adjacent to the feeder. Bird always walked to food and back into cover.Call: none heardPlumage: Probably 1st winter, unsure after Pyle
photographer: Photographer: Bill Maynard, bill maynard also for first one
